skill.name: agentdb-performance-optimization
skill.description: Optimize AgentDB vector database performance with quantization, HNSW indexing, caching, batch operations, monitoring, and scaling guidance.
skill.summary: Tune AgentDB for faster vector search, lower memory use, and better scaling.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static score is overstated because most Ruby backtick matches are Markdown code fences and TypeScript examples, not executable skill code. The confirmed risks are publishable with warnings: the guide recommends unpinned npx execution and local AgentDB database operations that may modify or inspect user data. No prompt injection, credential access, exfiltration flow, or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned External Package Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The guide tells users to run npx agentdb@latest for benchmarks and statistics. This is legitimate tooling, but it can fetch and execute the current npm package version, creating supply-chain and reproducibility risk if followed without pinning or review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and would execute an external package when copied. Confidence is high for operational risk, but there is no evidence of malicious command construction or secret exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Database Modification Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends optimization and pruning operations against AgentDB data. These examples support the advertised purpose, but users should back up databases and review thresholds before pruning patterns.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced operations are clearly local database maintenance examples. The risk is moderate because data may be changed, but the intent is performance tuning rather than destructive behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby backtick execution alerts correspond to fenced Markdown examples across SKILL.md. They are documentation snippets, not code that the skill executes automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Inspection confirms the matches are Markdown fenced examples. Some examples contain commands users may run, but the backtick pattern itself is a false positive.

low_findings.title: SQLite Path Alerts Are Example Database Paths
low_findings.description: The SQLite database matches are example AgentDB paths such as .agentdb/optimized.db and .agentdb/vectors.db. They do not expose credentials or embed a real database file in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are visible in examples and CLI snippets only. No database content, credential, or secret material appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Reference Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the AgentDB GitHub repository and website in a Learn More section. They are informational references, not network calls that transmit user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear as Markdown reference links at the end of the document. No code path sends data to those locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in descriptive performance text, not cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, signing, or random-number generation implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe AgentDB optimization and performance claims. I found no cryptographic API usage or algorithm recommendation on those lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Uses Latest Package Tag
dangerous_patterns.description: The npx examples use the latest package tag. Users should pin a reviewed version before running benchmarks or database statistics commands in production environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is confirmed and actionable. It is dangerous mainly through supply-chain exposure, not through evidence of malicious intent in the skill text.

content.value_statement: Vector databases can become slow or memory-heavy as embeddings grow. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical AgentDB tuning steps for search speed, memory use, and scale.
actual_capabilities: Compares binary, scalar, product, and full-precision quantization options for AgentDB.
Explains HNSW tuning parameters for recall, memory use, and search latency.
Provides cache sizing guidance for small, medium, and large applications.
Shows batch insert and parallel retrieval patterns for vector workloads.
Includes monitoring commands and metrics for AgentDB database performance.
Offers optimization recipes for speed, accuracy, memory limits, and scale.
limitations: Benchmark results depend on hardware, dataset shape, vector dimensions, and AgentDB version.
Examples assume Node.js and the agentic-flow AgentDB adapter are already available.
Quantization may reduce recall or ranking quality for some workloads.
The guide does not provide automated migration, backup, or rollback tooling.

use_cases.title: Scale embedding search
use_cases.description: Tune AgentDB settings when search latency rises as vector counts grow from thousands to millions.

use_cases.title: Reduce memory pressure
use_cases.description: Choose a quantization strategy that lowers memory use while preserving acceptable retrieval accuracy.

use_cases.title: Prepare production monitoring
use_cases.description: Track cache hit rate, database size, pattern counts, and latency before changing production settings.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a basic optimization profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my AgentDB workload size, memory limit, and accuracy needs. Recommend a safe starting configuration for quantization, cache size, and HNSW settings.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose slow search
prompt_templates.prompt: My AgentDB searches are slower than expected. Help me inspect cache behavior, HNSW parameters, vector count, and retrieval settings before changing production values.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a memory reduction rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a rollout plan for applying AgentDB quantization. Include benchmark steps, quality checks, rollback criteria, and monitoring metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Design a large-scale tuning strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an AgentDB optimization strategy for more than one million vectors. Balance HNSW build cost, recall, memory use, caching, and operational risk.

output_examples.input: I have 100,000 embeddings and memory usage is too high.
output_examples.output: The skill can recommend scalar or binary quantization, estimate memory savings, and list validation checks for retrieval quality.

output_examples.input: Search latency increased after my dataset grew.
output_examples.output: The skill can review cache size, HNSW search quality, batch retrieval, and benchmark metrics to identify likely bottlenecks.

output_examples.input: I need a production-safe optimization plan.
output_examples.output: The skill can outline benchmark, backup, rollout, monitoring, and rollback steps before changing AgentDB settings.

best_practices: Benchmark current latency, recall, cache hit rate, and database size before changing settings.
Back up local AgentDB databases before pruning, optimizing, or rebuilding indexes.
Pin package versions when running npx commands in controlled or production environments.
anti_patterns: Do not apply binary quantization without measuring retrieval quality on representative queries.
Do not tune cache size from memory limits alone without checking hit rate and workload patterns.
Do not run latest-tag external commands against production data without review and backups.

faq.question: Does this skill run AgentDB commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and example commands that a user or agent may choose to run.

faq.question: Which quantization option gives the largest memory reduction?
faq.answer: Binary quantization gives the largest stated reduction, but it can reduce retrieval accuracy.

faq.question: Can this skill improve HNSW search speed?
faq.answer: It explains HNSW parameters and tradeoffs so users can tune speed, recall, and memory use.

faq.question: Is it safe for production databases?
faq.answer: Use backups, pinned package versions, benchmarks, and rollback criteria before applying production changes.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What should I measure after optimization?
faq.answer: Measure search latency, recall quality, cache hit rate, memory use, database size, and insert latency.
